2605.12242.title: Mind the Pause: Disfluency-Aware Objective Tuning for Multilingual Speech Correction with LLMs
2605.12242.paper_summary: Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) transcripts often contain disfluencies, such as fillers, repetitions, and false starts, which reduce readability and hinder downstream applications like chatbots and voice assistants. If left unaddressed, such disfluencies can significantly degrade the reliability of downstream systems. Most existing approaches rely on classical models that focus on identifying disfluent tokens for removal. While this strategy is effective to some extent, it often disrupts grammatical structure and semantic coherence, leading to incomplete or unnatural sentences. Recent literature explored the use of large language models (LLMs); however, these efforts have primarily focused on disfluency detection or data augmentation, rather than performing comprehensive correction. We propose a multilingual correction pipeline where a sequence tagger first marks disfluent tokens, and these signals guide instruction fine-tuning of an LLM to rewrite transcripts into fluent text. To further improve reliability, we add a contrastive learning objective that penalizes the reproduction of disfluent tokens, encouraging the model to preserve grammar and meaning while removing disfluent artifacts. Our experiments across three Indian languages, namely Hindi, Bengali, and Marathi show consistent improvements over strong baselines, including multilingual sequence-to-sequence models. These results highlight that detection-only strategies are insufficient. Combining token-level cues with instruction tuning and contrastive learning provides a practical and scalable solution for multilingual disfluency correction in speech-driven NLP systems. We make the codes publicly available at https://github.com/deepak-kumar-98/Mind-the-Pause.
2605.12242.paper_summary_zh: 自動語音識別（ASR）轉錄通常包含流暢性問題，例如填充詞、重複和錯誤開始，這些問題會降低可讀性並妨礙下游應用程序，如聊天機器人和語音助手。若不加以處理，這些流暢性問題可能會顯著降低下游系統的可靠性。大多數現有方法依賴於傳統模型，專注於識別需移除的流暢性問題標記。雖然這一策略在某種程度上是有效的，但它往往會破壞語法結構和語義連貫性，導致不完整或不自然的句子。最近的文獻探討了使用大型語言模型（LLMs）；然而，這些努力主要集中在流暢性檢測或數據增強上，而不是進行全面的修正。我們提出了一個多語言修正管道，其中序列標記器首先標記流暢性問題標記，這些信號引導LLM的指令微調，以將轉錄重寫為流暢文本。為了進一步提高可靠性，我們增加了一個對比學習目標，懲罰流暢性問題標記的重現，鼓勵模型在移除流暢性問題的同時保留語法和意義。我們在三種印度語言上進行的實驗，即印地語、孟加拉語和馬拉地語，顯示出相對於強基準的一致改進，包括多語言序列到序列模型。這些結果突顯出僅依賴檢測的策略是不足夠的。將標記級提示與指令調整和對比學習相結合，為語音驅動的自然語言處理系統中的多語言流暢性修正提供了一個實用且可擴展的解決方案。我們將代碼公開提供於 https://github.com/deepak-kumar-98/Mind-the-Pause。
2605.12242.author: Deepak Kumar et.al.
2605.12242.authors: Deepak Kumar, Baban Gain, Asif Ekbal
